title: amaranthus carura
intro: Amaranthus carura, also known as Mexican love-lies-bleeding, is a species of flowering plant in the amaranth family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Amaranthus carura include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing skin irritations, making it a popular herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of Amaranthus carura include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil to create a topical cream.
